mcqs.question: What is the SI unit of velocity?
A body is said to be in motion when its position changes with respect to:
What does the slope of a distance-time graph represent?
Which of the following is a scalar quantity?
If a car travels 100m in 10s, its average speed is:

cqs.question: Define distance and displacement. How are they different from each other?
cqs.answer: Distance is the total path length traveled by an object regardless of direction. It is a scalar quantity. Displacement is the shortest straight-line distance between the initial and final positions of an object, measured in a specific direction. It is a vector quantity. Key difference: Distance can never be negative, but displacement can be negative, zero, or positive depending on direction.

cqs.question: A train travels 300 km in 3 hours. Calculate its average speed in m/s.
cqs.answer:
Given: Distance = 300 km = 300,000 m, Time = 3 hours = 10,800 s
Average Speed = Distance / Time
= 300,000 / 10,800
= 27.78 m/s
Therefore, the average speed of the train is approximately 27.78 m/s.

videos.title: Newton's First Law
videos.description: Understanding inertia and Newton's First Law of Motion.

mcqs.question: Newton's first law of motion is also called the law of:
The SI unit of force is:

cqs.question: State Newton's Three Laws of Motion and provide one example for each.
cqs.answer:
1st Law (Law of Inertia): An object at rest stays at rest and an object in motion stays in motion unless acted upon by a net external force. Example: A ball on a table remains still until pushed.

2nd Law (F = ma): The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force and inversely proportional to its mass. Example: Pushing a heavy box requires more force than a light one.

3rd Law (Action-Reaction): For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. Example: A rocket expels gas downward and moves upward.

videos.title: Introduction to Polynomials
videos.description: Learn what polynomials are and their types.

mcqs.question: What is the degree of the polynomial 3x² + 5x + 2?

cqs.question: What is a polynomial? Define degree of a polynomial.
cqs.answer:
A polynomial is an algebraic expression consisting of variables and coefficients, using only addition, subtraction, multiplication, and non-negative integer exponents. Example: 3x² + 2x + 1.

The degree of a polynomial is the highest power of the variable in the polynomial. For example, in 3x² + 2x + 1, the degree is 2.

videos.title: Atomic Structure Basics
videos.description: Introduction to atoms, protons, neutrons, and electrons.

mcqs.question: Who proposed the modern atomic theory?
The number of protons in an atom is called:

cqs.question: Explain Dalton's atomic theory and its limitations.
cqs.answer:
Dalton's Atomic Theory (1803) states:
1. All matter is made of tiny, indivisible particles called atoms.
2. Atoms of the same element are identical in mass and properties.
3. Atoms combine in simple whole number ratios to form compounds.
4. Chemical reactions involve rearrangement of atoms.

Limitations:
1. Atoms are NOT indivisible — they contain protons, neutrons, electrons.
2. Atoms of the same element can have different masses (isotopes).
3. Did not explain electrical nature of matter.
4. Did not account for allotropes.
